Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 


Ms Mary Ann Kvasager 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 
FPPC 
1100 K Street Building 
PO Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Ms Kvasager: 
2441 Carleton 
Berkeley, CA 94704 
23 June 1978 
Thank you for your helpful letter (6/14/78) explaining how to file as a 
Recipient Committee. 
I have completed and mailed Form 410 for our new group, 
the East Bay Area Coalition Against the Briggs Initiative (EBACABI). 
I am still confused by one matter--in your letter you state that our 
"first campaign statement due in connection with the proposed 
initiative must be filed not later than 35 days after the dead-
line for filing the petition or the date of notification that 
the initiative qualified or failed to qualify for the ballot, 
whichever is earlier." 
Does this mean that we have 35 calendar days to file our first campaign statement 
(Form 420)? 
How do I find out which is the earlier date for the Briggs Initiative 
(School Workers -- Homosexuality), and thus determine when our first statement 
is due? 
Am I correct in believing, then, that we have to file campaign disclosure 
statements four times: 
this first time I'm uncertain about, then on Sept. 30, 
Oct. 26 and Jan. 31? 
If you could answer these questions I'd be very greateful. 
Also, what happens 
if through my inexperience I've missed this first deadline? 
Thank you very much for your help. 
Sincerely yours, 
(Ms) 
Treasurer, EBACABI

sections.question: Does this mean that we have 35 calendar days to file our first campaign statement (Form 420)? How do I find out which is the earlier date for the Briggs Initiative (School Workers -- Homosexuality), and thus determine when our first statement is due? Am I correct in believing, then, that we have to file campaign disclosure statements four times: this first time I'm uncertain about, then on Sept. 30, Oct. 26 and Jan. 31? If you could answer these questions I'd be very greateful. Also, what happens if through my inexperience I've missed this first deadline?
sections.conclusion: I believe the confusion is with phrase "proposed initiative." The initiative your group OPPOSing has qualified for the November ballot. The question is whether or not the group qualified as A committee during the petition circulation phase. If the group qualified as a committee and actively opposed the petition circulation phase it should have filed a campaign statement on or before June 5, 1978, thirty-five days the deadline for qualification. If, on the other hand, the group did not participate in opposing qualification but has raised $500 or more for the purpose of OPPOSing the initiative on the November ballot, a semi-annual statement covering the period from the first reportable transaction through June 30, July 31, must be filed including all funds raised or spent. If the committee continues to be active in connection with the general election, the committee's next campaign statement is due September 30, the first pre-election statement in connection with the November election. The period covered by the first pre-election statement is July 1 through September 23. Regarding missed deadlines, a late filing can result in a penalty fee of $10 per day after the deadline until the statement is filed. Section 91013 states that liability need not be enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial basis he determines that the late filing was not willful and that enforcement of liability will not further the purposes of the Act.

sections.question_synthetic: The treasurer of EBACABI, an opposition committee to the Briggs Initiative, seeks clarification on campaign statement filing deadlines, specifically the 35-day rule, how to determine the applicable deadline date, the total number of required filings, and consequences for missed deadlines.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarifies that filing requirements depend on whether EBACABI qualified as a committee during petition circulation (requiring a June 5, 1978 filing) or only began fundraising after qualification (requiring semi-annual and pre-election statements). Late filings incur a $10/day penalty fee, though penalties may be waived if the late filing was not willful.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated 6/23/78) and FPPC response (dated 7/10/78). Extracted as advice_letter based on the FPPC response. Some OCR errors present ('OPPOSing', 'pnase', 'generqal', 'peJlalty') but content is legible. Response letter from Mary Ann Kvasager of Technical Assistance and Analysis Division.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does this mean that we have 35 calendar days to file our first campaign statement (Form 420)? How do I find out which is the earlier date for the Briggs Initiative (School Workers -- Homosexuality), and thus determine when our first statement is due? Am I correct in believing, then, that we have to file campaign disclosure statements four times: this first time I'm uncertain about, then on Sept. 30, Oct. 26 and Jan. 31? If you could answer these questions I'd be very greateful. Also, what happens if through my inexperience I've missed this first deadline?

CONCLUSION: I believe the confusion is with phrase "proposed initiative." The initiative your group OPPOSing has qualified for the November ballot. The question is whether or not the group qualified as A committee during the petition circulation phase. If the group qualified as a committee and actively opposed the petition circulation phase it should have filed a campaign statement on or before June 5, 1978, thirty-five days the deadline for qualification. If, on the other hand, the group did not participate in opposing qualification but has raised $500 or more for the purpose of OPPOSing the initiative on the November ballot, a semi-annual statement covering the period from the first reportable transaction through June 30, July 31, must be filed including all funds raised or spent. If the committee continues to be active in connection with the general election, the committee's next campaign statement is due September 30, the first pre-election statement in connection with the November election. The period covered by the first pre-election statement is July 1 through September 23. Regarding missed deadlines, a late filing can result in a penalty fee of $10 per day after the deadline until the statement is filed. Section 91013 states that liability need not be enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial basis he determines that the late filing was not willful and that enforcement of liability will not further the purposes of the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a treasurer's questions about campaign statement filing deadlines for an initiative opposition committee, clarifying the 35-day rule, applicable deadlines, required filing schedule, and late filing penalties.
